changes.newLimit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
changes.notes: Applies to all food sold in Singapore.
Verification required using the Food Additives Search tool or the List of Permitted Food Additives.
An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of an unpermitted additive.

changes.newLimit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
changes.notes: Applies to all food sold in Singapore.
Food businesses must ensure contaminants are within specified limits based on laboratory tests.
Prohibits import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing these specific compounds.

changes.newLimit: Must be declared on food labels
changes.notes: Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
changes.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
changes.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. These levels are legally permitted concentrations.

changes.newLimit: Compliance with Food Safety Basic Law
changes.notes: This law establishes the basic principles for ensuring food safety in Japan. Compliance with its principles and derived regulations is required.
This foundational law governs all aspects of food sanitation in Japan, including standards for ingredients, additives, contaminants, and labeling. Specific requirements must be verified.
